{"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-400", "name": "Uncontrolled Resource Consumption"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "## Impact

The undici WebSocket client enforces `maxPayloadSize` on the cumulative byte count of fragments in a message but does not enforce a limit on the number of fragments. A malicious WebSocket server can stream many small or empty continuation frames that each pass per-frame and cumulative-size validation, collectively causing unbounded memory growth in the client process. The result is memory exhaustion and a denial of service.

Affected applications are those using the undici WebSocket client (`new WebSocket(...)`) or the `WebSocketStream` API that can be induced to connect to an attacker-controlled or compromised WebSocket endpoint.

All releases starting at undici 6.17.0 are affected.

## Patches

Upgrade to undici v6.27.0, v7.28.0 or v8.5.0.

## Workarounds

No workaround is available. The fix must be applied through an upgrade.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00789, "percentile": 0.51977}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-vxpw-j846-p89q", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-19T14:22:01Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vxpw-j846-p89q", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-vxpw-j846-p89q"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-12151"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-17T17:16:42Z", "published_at": "2026-06-19T14:22:01Z", "references": ["https://github.com/nodejs/undici/security/advisories/GHSA-vxpw-j846-p89q", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-12151", "https://cna.openjsf.org/security-advisories.html", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-vxpw-j846-p89q"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/nodejs/undici/security-advisories/GHSA-vxpw-j846-p89q", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/nodejs/undici", "summary": "undici WebSocket client vulnerable to denial of service via fragment count bypass", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-19T14:23:29Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-vxpw-j846-p89q", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "6.27.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "undici"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 6.27.0"}, {"first_patched_version": "7.28.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "undici"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 7.0.0, < 7.28.0"}, {"first_patched_version": "8.5.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "undici"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 8.0.0, < 8.5.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: undici WebSocket client vulnerable to denial of service via fragment count bypass
